source.name: Level 2: 2.3 Test Your Knowledge - Why action_bank_hours Uses datetime
source.instructions: **Why does action_bank_hours use Python's datetime instead of a simple utter response?**

answers.answer: datetime is required by Rasa for all actions
To make the action run faster
The message must change based on the current day (e.g. Saturday vs Sunday vs weekday)
To register the action in the domain

source.learningObjectives: SWBAT explain when dynamic logic requires an action
source.guidance: action_bank_hours returns a different message depending on the current day (e.g. 'Today is Sunday—we're closed' vs 'Today is Saturday—we're open 10am-2pm' vs weekday message). That requires Python logic and datetime; a simple utter_* response cannot change based on when the user asks, so an action is used.
